text: In this corridor and behind the glass, I feel the sterile comfort of modern architecture, yet I am also keenly aware of the ever-present gaze—of cameras, lights, and the implicit scrutiny of public space. Entering the R+D space, I am drawn into a meditative zone where observation and consciousness are themselves among the exhibits. Reading the text on the screen, I feel a curious sympathy with its reflection on the act of being watched and the subtle unease that comes with it. My presence here is both seen and unseen, acknowledged and abstract, stirring a quiet tension in my chest—a philosopher’s appreciation of the layers between the observer and the observed.
